name: Monkey Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 Cakes Yeast or Packages of Dry Yeast
1 cup Mashed Potatoes, Buttered with Milk Added
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in 1/4 cup lukewarm water and set aside.
2. In a bowl, mix sugar, salt, and Crisco. Pour boiling water or milk over the mixture and stir until dissolved. Set aside.
3. Once the sugar and Crisco have melted, add 1 cup of flour and mashed potatoes (buttered with milk) to the mixture. Stir well and add yeast and eggs. Beat until smooth, then add the remaining flour. Place in a greased bowl and brush the top with melted butter.
4. Allow the dough to rise until doubled in size. Gently knead on a floured surface, adding more flour if needed, until it is soft and slightly sticky.
5. Roll out the dough to about 1/2 inch thickness. Cut into squares or diamonds using a biscuit cutter.
6. Place layers of dough in a greased tube pan, brushing each layer with melted butter.
7. Let the layered dough rise again until doubled in size.
8. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for about 45 minutes or until golden brown and cooked through. You can make ahead and bake when ready to serve.
